title: Cerumen recurrence review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports diagnosis only when clinician-observed cerumen is symptomatic or prevents assessment, plus history or examination of management-modifying factors.
examination.rationale: The exact modifying-factor section supports clinician-performed ENT examination and review of hearing or tympanometry information when available without authorising removal.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports diagnosis only when clinician-observed cerumen is symptomatic or prevents assessment, plus history or examination of management-modifying factors.

exact_section.heading: Diagnosis and modifying factors
exact_section.locator: Key Action Statements 2A–2B; official full-text lines 302–305 and 417–528

investigations.text: Supports cerumen-impaction identity, hearing loss, tinnitus, fullness, pain, discharge, otoscopy, ear-canal or tympanic-membrane visibility, and audiologic-test obstruction context.
investigations.rationale: The exact cerumen scope section supports interval symptom context, functional impact, hearing or discomfort features, and relevant negatives without asserting recurrent impaction.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports cerumen-impaction identity, hearing loss, tinnitus, fullness, pain, discharge, otoscopy, ear-canal or tympanic-membrane visibility, and audiologic-test obstruction context.

exact_section.heading: Scope, definition, and symptoms of cerumen impaction
exact_section.locator: Full guideline supporting text and Key Action Statement 2A; official full-text lines 183–217 and 417–475

referral.text: Supports documenting resolution after clinician treatment, considering alternate causes when symptoms persist after resolution, and clinician referral after unsuccessful initial management without mapping a procedure or treatment instruction.
referral.rationale: The exact outcomes section supports patient concerns, clinician-entered plan, safety net, arranged follow-up, and questions without selecting treatment or referral.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports documenting resolution after clinician treatment, considering alternate causes when symptoms persist after resolution, and clinician referral after unsuccessful initial management without mapping a procedure or treatment instruction.

exact_section.heading: Outcomes assessment and referral
exact_section.locator: Key Action Statements 9–10; official full-text lines 315–316 and 1137 onward

additions.text: Supports cerumen-impaction identity, hearing loss, tinnitus, fullness, pain, discharge, otoscopy, ear-canal or tympanic-membrane visibility, and audiologic-test obstruction context.
additions.rationale: The exact cerumen scope section supports interval symptom context, functional impact, hearing or discomfort features, and relevant negatives without asserting recurrent impaction.
additions.evidence_extract: Supports cerumen-impaction identity, hearing loss, tinnitus, fullness, pain, discharge, otoscopy, ear-canal or tympanic-membrane visibility, and audiologic-test obstruction context.

source_limitations: Recurrence count, laterality, hearing, pain, discharge, hearing aid, prior procedure, canal and membrane findings, diagnosis, removal method, outcome, referral, and prevention remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
